title: Relate Multiplication and Division
essential_question: How are multiplication and division related?
strategies: Use a multiplication fact to solve a division problem.
Multiplication and division are inverse operations — one undoes the other.

pretest.stem: Which multiplication fact helps you solve 63 ÷ 7?

learn.title: Inverse Operations
learn.content: Multiplication and division are inverse (opposite) operations. If 5 × 9 = 45, then 45 ÷ 5 = 9 and 45 ÷ 9 = 5.

learn.title: Think Multiplication
learn.content: To divide, think of the related multiplication. For 54 ÷ 6: think ? × 6 = 54. Since 9 × 6 = 54, then 54 ÷ 6 = 9.

learn.title: Division as Unknown Factor
learn.content: 32 ÷ 8 = ? is the same as asking ? × 8 = 32. The answer is 4, because 4 × 8 = 32.

hints: Think about what relate multiplication and division means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.
